_comment: Pinned qpdf release used by Phase 7.5 B8 password encryption. Diego owns. License: Apache-2.0 (Source URL: https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf, LICENSE.txt in tarball). Per-OS SHA256s are from the upstream PGP-signed manifest at https://github.com/qpdf/qpdf/releases/download/v11.9.1/qpdf-11.9.1.sha256 (signed by jberkenbilt, the qpdf maintainer). The fetch script (scripts/fetch-qpdf-binaries.mjs) verifies the archive SHA256 strictly and refuses to install a mismatched binary. Source for the layout details below: archive contents verified by manual extract on 2026-06-18.

_layoutNotes.win32-x64: msvc64 zip extracts to qpdf-11.9.1-msvc64/. bin/ contains qpdf.exe + the qpdf29.dll runtime + 7 Visual C++ runtime DLLs (concrt140, msvcp140, msvcp140_1, msvcp140_2, msvcp140_atomic_wait, msvcp140_codecvt_ids, vcruntime140, vcruntime140_1). All 9 DLLs are shipped alongside qpdf.exe because (a) qpdf.exe imports qpdf29.dll at startup, (b) qpdf29.dll imports the VC++ runtime DLLs, and (c) we can't depend on the user's machine having the VC++ Redistributable installed (Windows 10/11 Home editions often don't). Ship cost: ~7.5 MB total.
_layoutNotes.linux-x64: Linux zip extracts FLAT to bin/qpdf + lib/libqpdf.so.29.9.1 (with libqpdf.so.29 symlink) + 7 other system shared libs (libffi, libgnutls, libhogweed, libidn2, libjpeg, libnettle, libp11-kit, libtasn1, libunistring). The bin/qpdf binary uses relative RPATH/RUNPATH to find ../lib at runtime — so the bin/ + lib/ layout MUST be preserved in the packaged resources. Ship cost: ~9.5 MB total. CONFIG-ONLY / UNVERIFIED on real Linux hardware per electron-builder.yml Phase 7 P7-L-1 convention.
_layoutNotes.darwin: Upstream qpdf 11.9.1 publishes NO macOS prebuilt binary (verified 2026-06-18 from https://api.github.com/repos/qpdf/qpdf/releases/tags/v11.9.1 — assets are msvc/mingw Windows + linux-x86_64 + AppImage source + tarball source; no .dmg / no darwin zip). On macOS the binary discovery in src/main/pdf-ops/encryption-engine.ts (defaultQpdfBinaryPath) falls back to system PATH, typically populated by `brew install qpdf` (Apache-2.0 in Homebrew core). When neither is present the engine returns engine_unavailable with guidance. Documented limitation; tracked for Phase 7.6 if a permissive macOS binary source surfaces.
